summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a fire‑filled Ventura show highlighted by a volcanic Loser and a hypnotic Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Morning Dew sequence.
review: The recording is crisp and well balanced, with especially strong bass that lets the band's dynamics shine. Musically the second set is legendary – Jerry’s solo on “Loser” is described as a volcanic tidal‑wave, the percussion‑improv‑Morning Dew stretch and the Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Morning Dew suite are repeatedly praised, while a few listeners note the Ship of Fools feels a bit cold. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the best Ventura dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance and audio fidelity across >10 reviews; standout songs are repeatedly highlighted, with no serious complaints about the recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix with strong bass presence and good stereo separation; reviewers report no drop‑outs or noise issues.

standout_songs: Terrapin Station > Drums > Space > Morning Dew
I Just Want to Make Love to You (Brent cover)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is described as ‘fire’, with seamless transitions – especially the Percussion → Improvisation → Morning Dew stretch and the classic Terrapin‑Drums‑Space‑Dew sequence. Loser lands perfectly in the flow, though a few note Ship of Fools feels a bit jarring after Samson.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on “Loser” is called volcanic and emotionally tidal‑wave; his voice even likened to Louis Armstrong; never left stage between Not Fade Away and Brokedown Palace.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass levels are highlighted as awesome, providing a deep, driving foundation.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland delivers the only known funky rendition of Muddy Waters’ “I Want to Make Love to You,” noted as a unique highlight; keyboards described as funk‑filled.
